channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge orders Trump administration to tell fired workers they were not let go for poor performance
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A U.S. district judge in San Francisco on Friday ordered the Trump administration to provide probationary workers fired en masse a written statement saying they were not terminated for performance reasons, but as part of a government-wide termination.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump administration from passport changes affecting some transgender Americans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday partially blocked the Trump administration from enacting a policy that bans the use of “X” marker used by many nonbinary people on passports as well as the changing of gender markers. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Partisan divide widening on US support for NATO: Pew
item.description: A partisan divide is widening among Americans on the country's support for NATO, with Democrats being more likely to have a positive opinion of the military alliance than Republicans, according to a Thursday survey. The Pew Research Center poll found 66 percent of respondents think the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Ten Commandments, ‘In God We Trust’ in classrooms is now Arkansas law
item.description: Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders signed a bill into law this week making religious displays in classrooms state law.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, NewsNation

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: As Afghan Christians face deportation, faith leaders urge Trump administration to reconsider
item.description: Afghan Christians in the U.S. face imminent deportation after the termination of TPS, and faith leaders have made urgent appeals to the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans deny Democrats' requests for El Salvador oversight trips
item.description: House Republican committee chairs are denying Democrats’ requests to travel to El Salvador and visit its CECOT prison facility as scrutiny builds on the Trump administration’s handling of Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s mistaken deportation.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) and other conservative allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council (HSAC).
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says career gov employees working on policy will be fired if they don’t adhere to his agenda
item.description: President Trump on Friday said that federal career employees working on policy issues will be reclassified as ‘Schedule Policy/Career,’ or at-will.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge pauses Trump administration's plans for mass layoffs at Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: The decision leaves in limbo a bureau created after the Great Recession to safeguard against fraud, abuse and deceptive practices. Trump administration officials argue that it has overstepped its authority and should have a more limited mission.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Politics Friday: Digging into Minnesota‘s political history
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and a team of MPR News reporters and producers share a bit Minnesota political history with some modern twists.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Judge orders new limits on DOGE data access at Social Security Administration
item.description: A federal judge in Maryland granted a preliminary injunction that bars DOGE staffers from accessing non-anonymized personal data at the Social Security Administration.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Top Dem asks Noem how TSA will prevent 'major disruptions' as many travelers still lack REAL ID
item.description: Democrat Sen. Jack Reed of Rhode Island sent a letter to DHS Secretary Kristi Noem asking for a contingency plan in case travelers get stranded due to the REAL ID mandate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump calls FSU shooting a ‘shame,’ says he’ll always protect Second Amendment
item.description: President Trump on Thursday called a deadly shooting at Florida State University (FSU) "terrible," but he dismissed the possibility of it spurring any changes to gun laws in the country. "Look, I'm a big advocate of the Second Amendment. I have been from the beginning.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: LGBTQ Christians crusade against Trump's religiously 'hostile' policies during Holy Week
item.description: Attendees at the Collective of Queer Christian Leaders event in Washington told Fox News Digital they reject President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota license centers see long wait times as Real ID deadline nears
item.description: Real ID deadlines came and went for years. But now, with the actual deadline next month, people who weren't sure if the requirement would ever be implemented are now waiting hours to get their new IDs.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Poll position: How Trump's approval rating compares to his presidential predecessors
item.description: Where President Donald Trump's approval ratings stands in a new nation poll, and where he stands in comparison to his White House predecessors during their first three months in office
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Class action lawsuit challenges Trump’s crackdown on student visas
item.description: A federal judge who blocked Donald Trump’s administration from dismantling the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau has ruled that the agency can’t go forward immediately with plans to fire hundreds of employees.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with Abrego Garcia in El Salvador amid court fight over US return
item.description: SAN SALVADOR (AP) — Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen met with Kilmar Abrego Garcia on Thursday evening in El Salvador, coming face to face with the wrongly deported man after two days in the country pushing for his release.
item.dc:creator: YOLANDA MAGAÑA and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Career diplomat becomes the face of Trump's 'America First' agenda at the UN
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The highest-ranking U.S. representative now at the United Nations told Congress two years ago that Russia's invasion of Ukraine was “unprovoked" and “unjustified,” urging U.N. members to condemn Moscow’s aggression and demand an end to the war.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge in Baltimore temporarily limits DOGE access to Social Security data
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday imposed new restrictions on billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, limiting its access to Social Security systems that hold personal data on millions of Americans. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US citizen held by ICE despite judge seeing birth certificate
item.description: Juan Carlos Lopez-Gomez, who was born in the United States, was detained Wednesday in Florida by the state’s highway patrol and was charged with illegally entering the Sunshine State as an “unauthorized alien” under a state law that has been temporarily blocked by a judge, the Florida Phoenix news o
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has privately discussed firing Jerome Powell for months: Source
item.description: President Trump has privately discussed firing Jerome Powell for several months, a source confirms to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Pennsylvania senator asked child 'pornography' question during civics competition
item.description: Republican State Sen. David Argall asked the sixth grader if it was "appropriate to give pornographic magazines to kindergarteners.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota officials post strong March jobs report, note economic uncertainty ahead
item.description: Economic officials reported job growth in March but noted that mass layoff data likely isn’t represented yet.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Supervisor Lawson-Remer presents 'local battleplan' during State of County address
item.description: This year’s state of the county address was delivered in Balboa Park by acting Chair Terra Lawson-Remer. She shared her proposed plan for how to move the county forward in challenging fiscal times and what she considers “a moment of democratic crisis.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Gregorczyk, Fox 5

item.title: IRS asked to revoke Harvard's tax-exempt status
item.description: Only the IRS can review and revoke the existing status of taxpaying entities and has traditionally done so without presidential interference, as mandated by tax laws.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Florida House votes to stop pickleball courts, hotels from being built in state parks
item.description: A bill aiming to protect Florida's state parks from overdevelopment was unanimously passed Wednesday by the state House.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin squeaks past Minnesota for the top spot in voter turnout
item.description: The Minnesota Secretary of State confirms Wisconsin narrowly surpassed Minnesota with voter turnout in the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: NIL payments OK at UNLV, UNR, but payment amounts secret under SB293
item.description: A bill concerning NIL — name, image, likeness — and how UNLV and UNR can manage contracts directly with players passed on Tuesday in the Nevada Senate, advancing to the Assembly with other bills. A Republican leader objected to secrecy surrounding the payment amounts.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs executive action to lower drug prices
item.description: The action is intended to build upon the existing program for Medicare drug price negotiations, which was created by the Inflation Reduction Act that passed during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin, MPR News
